data.description:
# Background

The staff certification cases index (`CertificationCasesController#index` / `#closed`, rendered via the Strata `IndexComponent`) loads **every** matching case for the staff user's region in one query, then hydrates each with its `Certification`. On the AWS dev environment this page has become slow to load as case volume grows.

The query is currently unbounded and, for the **open** tab, **unordered**: the `open` scope is the enum-generated `where(status: :open)` with no `ORDER BY`. The apparent "oldest-first" ordering staff see is PostgreSQL heap return order, not application logic, so it is not guaranteed. (The `closed` scope, by contrast, is explicitly `order(created_at: :desc)` in the SDK.)

# Goal

Paginate both the open and closed tabs so each request loads and hydrates only one page of cases, and give the open tab a deterministic sort order.

# Design decisions (agreed)

- **Library: Pagy.** Lightweight, no ActiveRecord monkeypatching (keeps `CertificationCase` clean, consistent with the codebase's deliberate aggregate boundaries), zero meaningful runtime overhead. Kaminari's view-helper advantage is moot here because pagination controls must be hand-written as USWDS markup regardless.
- **Sort order: newest first** — `order(created_at: :desc, id: :desc)`. The `id` tiebreaker makes the order total so `OFFSET` paging is stable. This also aligns the open tab with the closed tab's existing intentional `created_at DESC` order.
- **Pagination must be applied before `CertificationService#hydrate_cases_with_certifications!`** so only the current page's certifications are loaded.
- **Controls live in a reusable USWDS partial** (`app/views/shared/_pagination.html.erb`) driven by the Pagy object, rendered around the untouched Strata `IndexComponent`. All strings go through i18n (en + es-US).

# Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Open and closed staff cases index tabs are paginated (default 25 per page).
- [ ] Open tab is ordered newest-first (`created_at DESC, id DESC`); paging is stable across requests (no repeated/skipped rows).
- [ ] Only one page of cases is loaded and hydrated per request (verified: no full-table load).
- [ ] Pagination controls use USWDS markup and are internationalized (en + es-US).
- [ ] Out-of-range `?page=` values are handled gracefully (clamp to last page).
- [ ] Request specs cover: page count, per-page limit, ordering, and an out-of-range page param.

# Follow-up / note

- For large regions the `COUNT` and page queries still hit the unindexed `status` column behind the region join. Consider an index supporting `(status, created_at)` (scoped appropriately) as a fast-follow; track separately if it grows beyond this story.
data.title: Add pagination to the staff certification cases index
